title: heliophila bulbostyla
intro: Heliophila bulbostyla, also known as the 'Heliophila', is a flowering plant species in the family Brassicaceae, commonly found in the southern regions of Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been utilized to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Heliophila bulbostyla include its ability to ease congestion, reduce inflammation, and promote overall respiratory well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or as a tincture, and are often consumed as a tea or used as a topical application for skin irritations.
